sourceMovie.title: Entrapment
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Entrapment works as a romance movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The romance and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Prestige
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Entrapment clicked for you, The Prestige (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Café Society
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Entrapment clicked for you, Café Society (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Entrapment?
faq.answer: Try The Prestige, Café Society, The Apartment—each shares DNA with Entrapment while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Entrapment?
faq.answer: Entrapment (1999) is best described as Romance, Drama, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Entrapment worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
